reasoning: To play in an NFL game before the end of 2025, Tom Brady faces multiple severe hurdles. First, he is a 47-year-old retired player who has not played since the 2022 season. Second, he recently signed to return to his primary job as the top NFL broadcaster for FOX in 2025. Most significantly, as of October 2024, he is officially a minority owner of the Las Vegas Raiders. Multiple sources from reliable outlets (Yahoo Sports, CBS Sports, AS, NBC Sports) confirm that as an owner, Brady cannot play in the NFL without obtaining explicit supermajority approval (likely 24 of 32 votes) from the other owners to un-retire and play while maintaining ownership, or he would have to sell his stake. Given the massive conflict of interest inherent in a partial owner of one team playing for either his own team or another franchise, rival owners have a strong incentive to block such a move. Brady himself has shown no real intent to undertake this near-impossible legal and practical reversal, instead solidifying his long-term post-football career in broadcasting and ownership. Therefore, the chance of him playing an NFL game before the end of 2025 is functionally 0, restricted only by the lower bound limit of 0.02.
